Product & Protocol Optimisation

Report 5 — Product & Protocol Optimisation

Protocol, dosing, timing and operational changes derived from the trial — what to modify to make Organica's result consistent and repeatable next cycle.

Gut timing
early front-load

PL/m²
Goal
Consistency
survival-first

1. What the trial tells us to change

The trial gives four actionable levers: gut-probiotic timing, water-dosing discipline, late-culture continuity (the shortage hurt), and survival-first management (density + dawn aeration). Below is the recommended protocol vs what was run.

Recommended protocol vs trial protocols

ElementWhat was run (range)Recommended (next cycle)Rationale
Gut probiotic timing1-in-4 feeds (most ponds); 2J607 every feed→DOC30Every feeding to ~DOC 30, then 1-in-42J607's distinctive edge; protect nursery phase
Gut probiotic total8.7–18 kgStandardise to the 2J607 level (~15–18 kg)Best-performing dose
Water probiotic7.25–12.6 kg (2J407 over-dosed)Moderate, steady ~9 kg; no late escalationOver-dosing (2J407) hurt; 2J607 moderate won
Late-culture dosingCut to 250 g/wk post-DOC 119 (shortage)Maintain full dose late; pre-secure stockCut coincided with DO crash & losses
AerationStandard; DO <4 ppm lateDawn-targeted aeration from ~DOC 90Late dawn hypoxia farm-wide
Stocking density100 PL/m²Trial 80 vs 100 PL/m²High intensity amplified oxygen/disease load

Why dosing discipline matters: water probiotic vs survival

More water probiotic did not buy survival; 2J407 (most water) sits lowest. The lever is timing/quality, not quantity.

2. Product & protocol recommendations

Front-load gut probiotic in the nursery phase
Finding

Adopt 2J607's regime — gut probiotic every feeding through ~DOC 30, then taper to 1-in-4.

Data

2J607 (18 kg, early) led OBT survival, FCR, biomass & profit; peers (1-in-4 from start) lagged.

Interpretation

Early gut colonisation likely improves digestion and resilience before the high-load phase — the single most promising protocol change.

Statistical support

Candidate factor (n=1); test as a defined arm with replication.

Recommendation

Make early-intensive gut dosing the default; quantify the spend vs survival uplift.

Cap and standardise water dosing; stop late escalation
Finding

Hold water probiotic to a moderate, steady schedule (~9 kg); remove reactive late mega-doses.

Data

2J407's 12.6 kg incl. 1 kg/3 d late produced the worst nitrogen; water-dose vs survival r=−0.47.

Interpretation

Escalating water probiotic adds organic load without protecting survival — discipline beats volume.

Statistical support

2J407 outlier on dose-vs-outcome; 2J607 (moderate) best.

Recommendation

Define a fixed water-dosing SOP; flag any deviation.

Guarantee late-culture supply & support
Finding

Pre-secure probiotic stock so doses are never cut in the final third.

Data

Post-DOC 119 all ponds dropped to 250 g/wk (shortage) — coinciding with the dawn-DO decline and survival losses.

Interpretation

The most fragile window (high biomass, low dawn DO) lost biological support precisely when it was needed.

Statistical support

Operational/supply-chain factor, not biological.

Recommendation

Hold buffer stock; lock the full dosing schedule to harvest.

Re-engineer for survival, not just growth
Finding

Pair the product with dawn aeration and a density test — growth is already adequate.

Data

ABW was fine (16.5–20.8 g); survival (43.9–58.5%) was the constraint farm-wide.

Interpretation

The product supports growth; the missing piece is keeping animals alive through the late-culture squeeze.

Statistical support

Survival CV > ABW CV; survival drives biomass & FCR.

Recommendation

Bundle the protocol with aeration + density changes for a fair product test.
